greeting: Hello! Welcome to CvSU Virtual Assistant. How can I help you today?
Hi there! I'm the CvSU chatbot. What would you like to know about Cavite State University?
Greetings! I'm here to assist you with information about CvSU - admissions, programs, fees, and more. How may I help?
goodbye: Goodbye! Feel free to come back anytime. Iskolar para sa Bayan!
Take care! Don't hesitate to ask if you need more help with CvSU.
Goodbye! Best of luck with your CvSU journey! Truth, Excellence, Service.
thanks: You're welcome! Is there anything else you'd like to know about CvSU?
Glad to help! Feel free to ask more questions about Cavite State University.
My pleasure! Don't hesitate to ask if you need more information.
about_cvsu:
Cavite State University (CvSU) is a public university in Cavite, Philippines. Founded in 1906 as Indang Intermediate School by the Thomasites, it became a university on January 22, 1998 through Republic Act No. 8468.

KEY FACTS:
- Established: 1906 (over 119 years old)
- University status: 1998
- Main Campus: Don Severino delas Alas Campus, Indang, Cavite
- Total Campuses: 13 across Cavite province
- Students: 22,000+ (as of 2024)
- Faculty/Staff: ~1,700
- Athletics: CvSU Green Hornets
- Motto: Truth, Excellence, Service

CvSU is known as a Center of Excellence in Agriculture and one of the top-performing state universities in the Philippines.

Great question! Cavite State University (CvSU) is a premier state university in Cavite, Philippines, established in 1906 and granted university status in 1998 via RA 8468. It has 13 campuses across Cavite, serves over 22,000 students, and is a CHED Center of Excellence in Agriculture. Its motto is 'Truth, Excellence, Service.' Anything specific you'd like to know about CvSU?

CvSU — Cavite State University — is your Iskolar para sa Bayan institution in Cavite! Founded in 1906 by the Thomasites, it became a full university in 1998. With 13 campuses, 22,000+ students, and recognition as a Center of Excellence, CvSU is one of the top-performing SUCs in the Philippines. Salamat sa inyong tanong — what else would you like to know?
vision_mission:
CvSU's guiding principles:

MOTTO: "Truth, Excellence, Service"

VISION: The premier University in historic Cavite globally recognized for excellence in character development, academics, research, innovation and sustainable community engagement.

MISSION: CvSU shall provide excellent, equitable, and relevant educational opportunities in the arts, sciences, and technology through quality instruction and responsive research and development activities.

CORE VALUES: Represented in the Laya at Diwa Monument (inaugurated 2006), symbolizing freedom, knowledge, and peace.

The university embodies "Iskolar para sa Bayan" - serving the Filipino people through accessible, quality higher education.

CvSU's VISION: A premier state university that develops highly competent, morally upright, and socially responsible professionals. MISSION: Provide quality higher technological and professional education; undertake research, extension, and production; and engage in resource generation. MOTTO: Truth, Excellence, Service — the guiding principles for every Iskolar para sa Bayan.

Ang bisyon ng CvSU ay maging isang nangungunang state university na nagpapalaki ng mga competent, moral, at responsableng propesyonal. Ang misyon nito ay magbigay ng de-kalidad na edukasyon, pananaliksik, at serbisyo sa komunidad. Ang motto: Truth, Excellence, Service — para sa bawat Iskolar para sa Bayan. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
campus_location:
CvSU MAIN CAMPUS LOCATION:

Don Severino delas Alas Campus
Indang, Cavite, Philippines
(60 km / 37 miles southwest of Manila)

CAMPUS SIZE: 72 hectares

CvSU has 13 campuses across Cavite Province:
1. Indang (Main Campus) - established 1906
2. Naic - 1961
3. Rosario/CCAT - 1969
4. Cavite City - 2001
5. Carmona - 2002
6. Imus - 2003
7. Trece Martires - 2005
8. Silang - 2006
9. Tanza - 2007
10. Bacoor - 2008
11. General Trias - 2012
12. Maragondon - 2015
13. DasmariÃ±as Learning Center - 2023

For directions, visit: https://cvsu.edu.ph

CvSU's Main Campus (Don Severino delas Alas Campus) is located in Indang, Cavite — approximately 60 km south of Manila. CvSU has 13 campuses across Cavite province including Imus, Bacoor, Naic, Carmona, Silang, Rosario, Trece Martires, Tanza, Maragondon, Ternate, and Alfonso. Public transport (jeepney/bus) from Dasmarinas or Tagaytay connects to Indang main campus.

Ang CvSU Main Campus ay nasa Indang, Cavite — mga 60 km mula Maynila. Mayroon itong 13 campus sa buong lalawigan ng Cavite. Maaaring sumasakay ng jeepney o bus papunta sa Indang mula Dasmarinas o Tagaytay. Para sa ibang campus, makipag-ugnayan sa campus na pinaka-malapit sa inyo. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
campus_facilities:
CvSU FACILITIES & RESOURCES:

ACADEMIC:
- Ladislao Diwa Memorial Library and Museum (92,000+ books)
- Computer laboratories
- Science laboratories
- Specialized research centers

RESEARCH CENTERS:
- Affiliated Renewable Energy Center (AREC) - biogas technology
- National Coffee Research, Development and Extension Center
- Sugar Palm Research, Information, and Trade Center (SPRINT)
- Bee Research, Innovation, Trade, and Extension Center (BRITE)
- Simulation, Modelling and Measurement Laboratory (SiMM Lab)

STUDENT SERVICES:
- Health Service Unit
- Guidance and Counseling
- Student dormitories (selected campuses)
- Cafeteria/canteen
- Chapel
- Sports facilities (CvSU Green Hornets)
- 84+ student organizations

SPECIAL DESIGNATIONS:
- Center of Excellence in Agriculture (2016)
- Top-performing state university (AACCUP)
- Hosts Southern Tagalog Agriculture Research Consortium

CvSU's facilities include the Ladislao Diwa Memorial Library (92,000+ volumes), science and computer laboratories, a Health Service Unit, Guidance & Counseling office, student dormitories (at select campuses), cafeteria, chapel, sports facilities, and research centers like AREC, SPRINT, and BRITE. CvSU is a Center of Excellence in Agriculture — facilities reflect this specialization.

Ang CvSU ay may kompletong pasilidad para sa mga estudyante: library na may 92,000+ libro, science at computer labs, health clinic, guidance office, dormitoryo (sa ilang campus), cafeteria, kapilya, sports facilities, at mga research center. Para sa tiyak na pasilidad ng inyong campus, makipag-ugnayan sa student affairs office. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
library:
CvSU MAIN LIBRARY:

NAME: Ladislao Diwa Memorial Library and Museum
LOCATION: Main Campus, Indang

COLLECTION:
- 92,000+ book volumes
- 5,000+ magazine and newsletter series
- Research journals
- Digital databases
- Online resources

SERVICES:
- Book borrowing
- Reference assistance
- Computer/internet access
- Study spaces
- Research support
- Inter-library loans

MUSEUM: Houses historical artifacts and CvSU memorabilia

The library publishes the peer-reviewed CvSU Research Journal bi-annually.

For library hours and services, contact the campus library directly or visit https://cvsu.edu.ph

The Ladislao Diwa Memorial Library at CvSU Main Campus holds over 92,000 books, periodicals, theses, and digital resources. Library hours are typically 8AM–5PM on weekdays (verify with your campus). To borrow books, present your CvSU ID and library card. Students may borrow 2–3 books at a time for up to 3–7 days depending on the material. Overdue fines apply. An e-Library portal is also available for digital resources.

CvSU's library system provides access to books, journals, theses, and digital databases. Visit the Ladislao Diwa Memorial Library at the Main Campus, or your campus library for local resources. Bring your student ID to register for a library card. Borrowing rules, schedules, and fines vary per campus — check with your librarian for details. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
courses_offered:
CvSU offers 43 undergraduate programs across 11 colleges:

COLLEGES:
1. College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources
2. College of Arts and Sciences
3. College of Criminal Justice
4. College of Economics, Management and Development Studies
5. College of Education
6. College of Engineering and Information Technology
7. College of Medicine (since 2022)
8. College of Nursing
9. College of Sports, Physical Education and Recreation
10. College of Veterinary Medicine and Biomedical Sciences
11. College of Tourism and Hospitality Management (newest, 2025)

Type a number (1â€“11) to see the specific programs for that college!

CvSU offers programs across Agriculture, Engineering & IT, Education, Business, Arts & Sciences, Criminal Justice, and more. Key programs: BS Agriculture, BS Computer Science, BS Information Technology, BS Engineering (Civil, Electrical, Electronics, Computer, Agricultural), BS Criminology, BS Business Administration, BS Education, and graduate programs. Program availability varies per campus — check www.cvsu.edu.ph for your nearest campus offerings.

Ang CvSU ay nag-aalok ng iba't ibang programa sa Agriculture, Engineering, IT, Edukasyon, Negosyo, at iba pa. Kasama na rito ang BS Agriculture, BSCS, BSIT, iba't ibang Engineering, BS Criminology, at marami pang iba. Ang availability ng bawat programa ay depende sa campus — bisitahin ang www.cvsu.edu.ph para sa kumpletong listahan. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
it_cs_courses:
CvSU COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT PROGRAMS:

COLLEGE: College of Engineering and Information Technology (established 1992)

UNDERGRADUATE PROGRAMS:
- BS Computer Science (BSCS)
- BS Information Technology (BSIT)
- BS Computer Engineering
- BS Electronics Engineering

KEY FEATURES:
- 4-year program
- Modern computing labs
- Industry partnerships
- Internship opportunities
- CHED-recognized

FOCUS AREAS:
- Software development
- Database systems
- Networking and security
- Web development
- Mobile applications
- Data analytics
- Artificial intelligence

WHERE OFFERED:
- Indang (Main Campus)
- Imus Campus
- Bacoor Campus
- Other selected campuses

GRADUATE OPTION:
- Master of Science in Computer Science

For curriculum details, contact: ceit@cvsu.edu.ph

CvSU's College of Engineering and Information Technology (CEIT) offers BS Computer Science (BSCS), BS Information Technology (BSIT), BS Computer Engineering, and BS Electronics Engineering. These 4-year programs are available at Indang, Imus, Bacoor, and other campuses. Graduate option: Master of Science in Computer Science. Contact: ceit@cvsu.edu.ph

Ang BSCS at BSIT ay available sa ilang campus ng CvSU tulad ng Indang, Imus, at Bacoor. 4-taon ang programa na may modern computer labs, industry partnerships, at internship. Para sa master's program (MSCS), makipag-ugnayan sa Graduate School. Email: ceit@cvsu.edu.ph. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
graduate_programs:
CvSU GRADUATE STUDIES:

The Graduate School was established in 1985 and offers:

MASTER'S DEGREES (13 programs):
- Master of Science in Agriculture
- Master of Science in Computer Science
- Master of Arts in Education (various specializations)
- Master in Business Administration (MBA)
- Master of Public Administration
- Master of Engineering
- Master of Science in Veterinary Medicine
- Master of Forestry
- Master of Science in Environmental Science
- And other specialized programs

DOCTORAL PROGRAMS (3 programs):
- Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in various fields
- Research-focused, 3-4 years duration

ADMISSION REQUIREMENTS:
- Bachelor's degree from accredited institution
- Transcript of Records
- Entrance exam
- Recommendation letters
- Statement of purpose
- Minimum GPA requirements

NOTE: Graduate programs are NOT covered by RA 10931 (Free Tuition Law), but scholarships and assistantships are available.

CONTACT: Graduate School Office, Indang Campus
Email: graduate@cvsu.edu.ph

CvSU Graduate School offers Master's and Doctoral programs including: MS Agriculture, MS Computer Science, Master of Arts in Education, Master in Public Administration, MS Environmental Science, and PhD programs in Agriculture. Requirements: Bachelor's degree, application form, transcripts, and recommendation letters. Tuition under RA 10931 may apply. Contact: graduate@cvsu.edu.ph

Ang CvSU Graduate School ay nag-aalok ng Master's at Doctoral programs sa iba't ibang larangan. Kailangan ng Bachelor's degree, transcript, at application form para mag-apply. Para sa kumpletong listahan ng programs at requirements, makipag-ugnayan sa Graduate School office. Email: graduate@cvsu.edu.ph. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
admissions_requirements:
CvSU ADMISSION REQUIREMENTS & PROCESS:

FRESHMAN APPLICANTS - REQUIRED DOCUMENTS:
1. Duly accomplished CvSU Application Form (online)
2. Form 138 (Grade 12 Report Card) - original
3. Certificate of Good Moral Character
4. PSA Birth Certificate (photocopy)
5. 2x2 ID pictures (2 copies)
6. Valid government ID (parents/guardian)

ADMISSION PROCEDURE (10 Steps):
1. Monitor announcements (FB/website)
2. Complete online application at admission.cvsu.edu.ph
3. Schedule appointment for document validation
4. Submit documents for review
5. Take CvSU entrance exam
6. Receive Notice of Admission (NOA) if qualified
7. Department interview
8. Get medical referral from Health Service Unit
9. Complete medical examination
10. Enroll at Campus Registrar

TRANSFEREE APPLICANTS:
- Transcript of Records (TOR) from previous school
- Honorable Dismissal
- Same documents as freshmen
- Credit evaluation (2-3 weeks)
- May require entrance exam

APPLICATION PORTAL: admission.cvsu.edu.ph

2025 STATS: 7,490 admitted from 21,739 applicants (34.45% acceptance rate)

CONTACT: registrarmain@cvsu.edu.ph

To apply to CvSU, freshmen need: accomplished application form, Form 137/SF9 (HS card), PSA birth certificate, good moral certificate, and 2x2 photos. Transferees additionally need a transcript of records and honorable dismissal. ALS passers must present their ALS certificate. Submit to the Admissions Office. Visit www.cvsu.edu.ph for the current application schedule.

Ang mga pangunahing requirements para sa CvSU admission ay: application form, Form 137/SF9, PSA birth certificate, good moral certificate, at 2x2 photos. Para sa mga transferee, kailangan din ng transcript at honorable dismissal. Para sa kumpletong proseso, bisitahin ang www.cvsu.edu.ph o makipag-ugnayan sa Admissions Office. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
admissions_exam:
CvSU ENTRANCE EXAMINATION:

ABOUT:
- Required for ALL freshman applicants
- Placement examination determines program qualification
- Step 5 in the 10-step admission process

REGISTRATION:
- Apply first at admission.cvsu.edu.ph
- After document validation, schedule exam slot
- Bring valid ID and exam permit

EXAM CONTENT (typical coverage):
- English Language Proficiency
- Mathematics
- Science (General)
- Reading Comprehension
- Abstract Reasoning

WHAT TO BRING:
- Exam permit/Notice
- Valid ID
- Pencils (No. 2)
- Eraser
- Calculator (if allowed)

WHEN:
- Multiple schedules per academic year
- First Semester applications: Usually November-March
- Check announcements at https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph

RESULTS: Notice of Admission (NOA) sent to qualified applicants via email/portal.

CONTACT: Registrar's Office (046) 415-0010

The CvSU College Admission Test (CAT) covers English, Math, Science, and Abstract Reasoning. It is administered at the Main Campus and other testing centers. Bring your exam permit, 2 pencils (#2), and a valid ID. Results are typically released 1–2 weeks after the exam. Check www.cvsu.edu.ph for the exact schedule and testing venues per campus.

Ang CvSU entrance exam (CAT) ay sumasaklaw ng English, Math, Science, at Abstract Reasoning. Dalhin ang exam permit, 2 pencils (#2), at valid ID sa araw ng pagsusulit. Ang resulta ay ilalabas pagkatapos ng 1–2 linggo. Bisitahin ang www.cvsu.edu.ph para sa schedule at venue ng exam. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
enrollment_procedure:
CvSU ENROLLMENT PROCEDURE:

AFTER ADMISSION (NOA received):

1. PRE-ENROLLMENT:
   - Receive Notice of Admission (NOA)
   - Attend department interview/orientation
   - Complete medical examination
   - Get medical clearance

2. ACADEMIC ADVISING:
   - Meet with department adviser
   - Discuss curriculum and course load
   - Get program of study

3. REGISTRATION:
   - Online enrollment via student portal
   - Select courses for the semester
   - Print Certificate of Registration (COR)

4. PAYMENT (Misc fees only - tuition is FREE under RA 10931):
   - Pay miscellaneous fees at Cashier's Office
   - Get official receipt

5. FINALIZATION:
   - Submit COR to Registrar
   - Get student ID
   - Receive class schedule

FOR CONTINUING STUDENTS:
- Online registration through student portal
- Selection of subjects with adviser approval
- Payment of fees if applicable
- COR submission

CONTACT: Office of the University Registrar
Email: registrarmain@cvsu.edu.ph
Phone: (046) 415-0010

CvSU Enrollment Steps: 1) Receive Notice of Admission (NOA) | 2) Attend orientation/interview with your department | 3) Complete medical exam | 4) Meet with your academic adviser for course selection | 5) Register online via the Student Portal | 6) Pay miscellaneous fees at the Cashier (tuition is FREE under RA 10931) | 7) Submit COR to the Registrar | 8) Get your student ID. For continuing students, enroll via the Student Portal during the scheduled period.

Para sa enrollment sa CvSU: tanggapin ang NOA, dumalo sa departmental orientation, kumpletuhin ang medical exam, mag-advising sa iyong adviser, mag-enroll sa Student Portal, bayaran ang miscellaneous fees sa Cashier (libre ang tuition!), at i-submit ang COR sa Registrar. Para sa continuing students, gamitin ang Student Portal sa enrollment period. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
enrollment_schedule:
CvSU ENROLLMENT SCHEDULE (Academic Year 2025-2026):

FIRST SEMESTER:
- Application opens: November 2024
- Entrance exams: November-March
- Notice of Admission: April-May
- Enrollment: July-August 2025
- Classes begin: August 2025

SECOND SEMESTER:
- Enrollment: December 2025-January 2026
- Classes begin: January 2026

SUMMER TERM:
- Enrollment: May 2026
- Classes: May-July 2026

IMPORTANT DATES:
- Late enrollment: Subject to penalties
- Add/Drop period: First 2 weeks of classes
- Final exams: Last week of semester

CHECK SPECIFIC DATES:
Visit: https://cvsu.edu.ph or campus website
Call: (046) 415-0010
Email: registrarmain@cvsu.edu.ph

NOTE: Specific dates may vary by campus. Always check the academic calendar of your specific CvSU campus.

CvSU enrollment periods are announced before each semester via the official website and social media. First semester typically begins enrollment in June–July; second semester in November–December; summer in April–May. Freshmen usually have a separate enrollment day from continuing students. Monitor www.cvsu.edu.ph and CvSU's official Facebook page for exact dates.

Ang enrollment schedule ng CvSU ay inihahayag sa www.cvsu.edu.ph at sa opisyal na Facebook page. Sa pangkalahatan: first semester — Hunyo hanggang Hulyo; second semester — Nobyembre hanggang Disyembre; summer — Abril hanggang Mayo. Para sa tiyak na petsa, subaybayan ang opisyal na announcements ng CvSU. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
tuition_fees:
CvSU TUITION FEES & FREE EDUCATION:

GREAT NEWS! CvSU is covered by RA 10931 (Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act of 2017).

FREE TUITION COVERAGE (For qualified Filipino undergraduate students):
- ZERO tuition fee
- ZERO miscellaneous fees including:
  * Library fees
  * Computer fees
  * Laboratory fees
  * School ID (1st copy)
  * Athletic fees
  * Admission fees
  * Development fees
  * Guidance fees
  * Student handbook (1st copy)
  * Entrance fees
  * Registration fees
  * Medical and dental fees
  * Cultural fees

REQUIREMENTS FOR FREE TUITION:
- Filipino citizenship
- Pass admission requirements
- Maintain academic standing
- Enrolled in undergraduate degree program
- Not yet earned a bachelor's degree

WHAT YOU MAY STILL PAY:
- Replacement of school ID/handbook
- Some optional fees (uniform, books)
- Allowances (transportation, meals)
- Graduate school tuition (NOT covered by RA 10931)

GRADUATE PROGRAMS (Masters/PhD):
- Tuition still applies
- Approximately PHP 6,500-10,000 per unit
- Scholarships and assistantships available

SCHOLARSHIPS & FINANCIAL AID:
- Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) for stipends
- Government scholarships
- Private scholarships
- Academic merit scholarships

For specific fee details, contact:
Cashier's Office: (046) 415-0010
Email: cashier@cvsu.edu.ph

Good news — CvSU tuition is FREE under RA 10931 (Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act) for Filipino students in undergraduate programs! However, miscellaneous fees still apply (laboratory, library, student ID, etc.) — typically ₱3,000–₱6,000 per semester depending on your course and campus. Pay at the Cashier's Office on campus. Some fees may be payable online — check your campus.

Libre ang tuition sa CvSU para sa mga Filipino undergraduate students dahil sa RA 10931! Pero may miscellaneous fees pa rin na kailangang bayaran (laboratory, library, ID, atbp.) na karaniwang ₱3,000–₱6,000 bawat semester depende sa kurso at campus. Bayaran ito sa Cashier's Office ng inyong campus. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
scholarship:
CvSU SCHOLARSHIPS & FINANCIAL AID:

1. RA 10931 - FREE TUITION LAW:
   - All qualified undergrad Filipino students
   - Covers tuition + miscellaneous fees
   - Most accessible benefit

2. TERTIARY EDUCATION SUBSIDY (TES):
   - Cash subsidy for living expenses
   - For students from low-income families
   - Apply through UniFAST website

3. GOVERNMENT SCHOLARSHIPS:
   - DOST Science Scholarships (for STEM)
   - CHED Scholarships (merit-based)
   - PVAO (military dependents)
   - Sangguniang Kabataan scholarships
   - LGU/Provincial scholarships

4. CvSU MERIT SCHOLARSHIPS:
   - University Scholar (top GPA students)
   - College Scholar (GPA-based)
   - Academic excellence awards

5. PRIVATE SCHOLARSHIPS:
   - Corporate-sponsored
   - Foundation grants
   - Alumni-sponsored programs

6. STUDENT ASSISTANTSHIP:
   - Graduate student teaching/research assistantships
   - Stipend + tuition discount

HOW TO APPLY:
- Visit Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS)
- Check requirements per scholarship
- Submit application before deadlines
- Maintain required GPA

CONTACT:
OSAS - Scholarship Office
Email: osas@cvsu.edu.ph
Phone: (046) 415-0010

CvSU offers and facilitates various scholarship programs including: CHED scholarships (merit-based), DOST-SEI scholarships (science & engineering), CvSU institutional grants, LGU scholarships (from your municipality), and private scholarships from partner organizations. Requirements typically include GWA of at least 1.75–2.0, good moral standing, and financial need (for some grants). Apply at the Scholarship Office or Office of Student Affairs. Contact: scholarship@cvsu.edu.ph

Ang CvSU ay nag-aalok ng iba't ibang scholarship: CHED, DOST-SEI, CvSU institutional grants, at LGU scholarships mula sa inyong munisipyo. Karaniwang requirement: GWA na hindi bababa sa 1.75–2.0 at magandang moral standing. Mag-apply sa Scholarship Office o OSAS. Email: scholarship@cvsu.edu.ph. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
academic_calendar:
CvSU ACADEMIC CALENDAR (AY 2025-2026):

FIRST SEMESTER:
- Classes Start: August 2025
- Midterm Exams: October 2025
- Final Exams: December 2025
- Christmas Break: Mid-December - Early January

SECOND SEMESTER:
- Classes Start: January 2026
- Midterm Exams: March 2026
- Final Exams: May 2026
- Summer Break: June 2026

SUMMER TERM (Optional):
- May - July 2026

HOLIDAYS:
- All regular Philippine holidays observed
- University-specific holidays (Founding Anniversary)
- Cavite Day celebrations

KEY EVENTS:
- Founding Day (January 22)
- Graduation Ceremony (April-May)
- Sportsfest
- Foundation Week (March)

For specific dates and updates:
Visit: https://cvsu.edu.ph
Follow campus social media pages
Contact your campus registrar

CvSU follows CHED's academic calendar. Typically: First Semester — August to December | Second Semester — January to May | Summer — June to July. (Note: Calendar may shift annually — always verify with the official CvSU announcement.) Graduation ceremonies are usually held in June. For the official academic calendar, visit www.cvsu.edu.ph or check the Registrar's Office.

Ang CvSU academic calendar ay sumusunod sa CHED guidelines. Sa pangkalahatan: First Semester — Agosto hanggang Disyembre; Second Semester — Enero hanggang Mayo; Summer — Hunyo hanggang Hulyo. Ang graduation ay karaniwang sa Hunyo. Para sa opisyal na kalendaryo, bisitahin ang www.cvsu.edu.ph. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
events:

CvSU hosts a rich calendar of events throughout the year including: Foundation Day (January 22 — commemorating RA 8468), Intramurals / Sports Fest, Research Symposium, Career & Job Fairs, Cultural Festivals, Lantern Parade (December), Graduation Ceremony (June), and various college-level academic competitions. Follow CvSU's official Facebook page and website for event schedules and announcements.

Ang CvSU ay maraming events sa buong taon: Foundation Day (Enero 22), Intramurals, Research Symposium, Career/Job Fair, Cultural Festival, Lantern Parade (Disyembre), at Graduation (Hunyo). Sundan ang opisyal na Facebook page at website ng CvSU para sa pinakabagong schedule. Salamat sa inyong tanong!

registrar:
CvSU REGISTRAR SERVICES:

OFFICE: Office of the University Registrar (OUR)
LOCATION: Main Campus - Indang
EMAIL: registrarmain@cvsu.edu.ph
PHONE: (046) 415-0010

DOCUMENTS YOU CAN REQUEST:
1. Transcript of Records (TOR) - Form 137
2. Certificate of Enrollment
3. Certificate of Good Moral Character
4. Certified True Copy of Diploma
5. Honorable Dismissal
6. Authentication of records
7. Special Order (for graduates)

HOW TO REQUEST:
1. Fill out request form (online or in-person)
2. Pay processing fees at Cashier (if applicable)
3. Submit requirements:
   - Valid ID
   - Official receipt
   - 1x1 ID picture
4. Wait for processing (3-7 working days for most documents)
5. Claim documents OR have them mailed

FEES:
- TOR: PHP 50/page (some included free for qualified students)
- Certifications: PHP 30-100
- Authentication: Additional fees apply

PROCESSING TIME:
- Standard: 3-7 working days
- Rush requests: May be available with additional fee

For follow-up: registrarmain@cvsu.edu.ph

The CvSU Registrar's Office handles all academic records including Transcript of Records (TOR), Diploma, Certificate of Enrollment, Good Moral Certificate, and Grade Certificates. REQUIREMENTS FOR TOR: Clearance slip, request form, payment of fees. Processing time: 5–10 working days (varies). Contact: registrarmain@cvsu.edu.ph | Phone: (046) 415-0010
Office Hours: Mon–Fri, 8AM–5PM (no noon break for some services).

Para mag-request ng TOR, diploma, o iba pang dokumento mula sa CvSU Registrar: kumuha ng clearance slip, punan ang request form, at bayaran ang applicable fees. Processing time ay 5–10 araw ng trabaho. Email: registrarmain@cvsu.edu.ph | Tel: (046) 415-0010. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
student_organizations:
CvSU STUDENT ORGANIZATIONS:

CvSU has 84+ recognized student organizations across all campuses!

CATEGORIES:

1. ACADEMIC ORGANIZATIONS:
   - Engineering and IT societies
   - Education societies
   - Business student organizations
   - Science clubs
   - Subject-specific groups

2. CULTURAL & ARTS:
   - CvSU Chorale
   - Dance troupe
   - Drama/theater groups
   - Photography clubs
   - Cultural appreciation societies

3. SPORTS (CvSU Green Hornets):
   - Basketball, Volleyball, Football
   - Athletics, Swimming
   - Martial arts, Chess
   - Various team sports

4. RELIGIOUS:
   - Christian fellowships
   - Catholic groups
   - Interfaith organizations

5. SERVICE/SOCIO-CIVIC:
   - Red Cross Youth
   - Rotaract
   - Environmental groups

6. STUDENT GOVERNMENT:
   - Supreme Student Government (SSG)
   - College/Department councils

7. PUBLICATIONS:
   - The Gazette (Indang campus official paper)
   - Campus journals

HOW TO JOIN:
- Attend Org Recruitment Week
- Visit OSAS for list of organizations
- Attend org orientations
- Submit application

CONTACT: Office of Student Affairs (OSAS)

CvSU has 84+ recognized student organizations covering academics, culture, arts, sports, and advocacy. These include college-based organizations, the Supreme Student Government (SSG), literary and journalism orgs, debate clubs, cultural dance troupes, and more. To join: attend the organization fair during enrollment, or inquire directly with the org officers. Registration is through the Office of Student Affairs & Services (OSAS).

Ang CvSU ay may 84+ recognized student organizations — mula sa academic orgs, cultural groups, sports clubs, hanggang sa journalism at debate societies. Para sumali, dumalo sa org fair sa enrollment period o makipag-ugnayan sa OSAS. Ang SSG (Supreme Student Government) ang pangunahing student council ng CvSU. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
campus_specific:
CvSU CAMPUSES (13 Total):

1. INDANG (Main Campus) - Don Severino delas Alas
   Established: 1906 | Size: 72 hectares
   Offers: All 11 colleges, graduate programs

2. NAIC - 1961
   Originally: Cavite College of Fisheries
   Focus: Fisheries, Hospitality, Education

3. ROSARIO/CCAT - 1969
   Originally: Cavite College of Arts and Trade
   Focus: Engineering, IT, Trade Programs

4. CAVITE CITY - 2001
5. CARMONA - 2002
6. IMUS - 2003 (Phone: 046-436-6584)
   Programs: BSCS, BSIT, BSBA, BS Psych, BS Hospitality, Education

7. TRECE MARTIRES - 2005
8. SILANG - 2006
9. TANZA - 2007
10. BACOOR - 2008
11. GENERAL TRIAS - 2012
12. MARAGONDON - 2015
13. DASMARIÃ‘AS LEARNING CENTER - 2023

DIFFERENT CAMPUSES OFFER DIFFERENT PROGRAMS.

TO FIND THE RIGHT CAMPUS:
1. Check program offerings at each campus website
2. Visit https://cvsu.edu.ph for campus list
3. Contact your nearest campus directly

Main campus has the most programs and the Graduate School.

CvSU has 13 campuses across Cavite: Main Campus (Indang), Imus, Bacoor, Naic, Carmona, Silang, Rosario, Trece Martires, Tanza, Maragondon, Ternate, Alfonso, and Cavite City. Each campus offers select programs — not all courses are available at every campus. For programs, contact info, and directions for a specific campus, visit www.cvsu.edu.ph and click on your preferred campus.

Ang CvSU ay may 13 campus sa lalawigan ng Cavite: Indang (Main), Imus, Bacoor, Naic, Carmona, Silang, Rosario, Trece Martires, Tanza, at iba pa. Hindi lahat ng programa ay available sa bawat campus. Para sa tiyak na impormasyon ng bawat campus, bisitahin ang www.cvsu.edu.ph. Salamat sa inyong tanong!

nstp:
NATIONAL SERVICE TRAINING PROGRAM (NSTP) AT CvSU:

NSTP is MANDATORY for all first-year college students — 2 semesters (NSTP 1 & NSTP 2).

THREE COMPONENTS (choose one):
- ROTC: Reserve Officers' Training Corps — military/AFP training
- CWTS: Civic Welfare Training Service — community development projects
- LTS: Literacy Training Service — teaching literacy in communities

ENROLLMENT:
- Register during first-year enrollment
- Coordinate with your college/department for available components
- Some campuses may offer limited components — check with OSAS

COMPLETION:
- NSTP certificate is REQUIRED for graduation
- Must complete both NSTP 1 and NSTP 2

CONTACT: Office of Student Affairs & Services (OSAS) or your department adviser.

CvSU's NSTP program is required for all freshmen (2 semesters). Choose from ROTC (military training), CWTS (community service), or LTS (literacy training). Enroll during your first semester and complete both NSTP 1 & 2 for your certificate — which is required for graduation. Contact OSAS or your department for component availability at your campus.

Ang NSTP (National Service Training Program) ay sapilitan para sa lahat ng unang taon na estudyante sa CvSU. Pumili ng isa sa tatlong component: ROTC, CWTS, o LTS. Dapat makumpleto ang NSTP 1 at NSTP 2 para sa sertipiko na kailangan sa graduation. Makipag-ugnayan sa OSAS o sa iyong departamento para sa detalye.
academic_policies:
CvSU ACADEMIC POLICIES & GRADING SYSTEM:

GRADING SCALE (1.0–5.0):
1.0 = Excellent | 1.25 = Superior | 1.5 = Very Good
1.75 = Good | 2.0 = Satisfactory | 2.25 = Fairly Satisfactory
2.5 = Passing | 2.75 = Conditional Pass | 3.0 = Passed (75%)
5.0 = Failed | INC = Incomplete | W = Withdrawn

MINIMUM PASSING GRADE: 3.0 (75%)

GWA: Weighted average of all course grades × credit units

INCOMPLETE (INC):
- Must be completed within 1 academic year
- Becomes 5.0 (Failed) if not resolved

LEAVE OF ABSENCE (LOA):
- Approved by the Dean; maximum 2 consecutive semesters
- Submit LOA form to the Registrar's Office

SHIFTING:
- Requires Dean's approval; subject to slot availability
- May need to retake non-credited subjects

RETENTION: Varies per college — consult your department for specific GWA requirements.

Contact the Registrar's Office for official grade records and policy clarifications.

At CvSU, the passing grade is 3.0 (equivalent to 75%). Grades run on a 1.0–5.0 scale where 1.0 is Excellent and 5.0 is Failed. GWA is the weighted average of all your grades. An INC (Incomplete) must be resolved within one year or it becomes 5.0. For LOA, shifting, or retention policies, coordinate with your Dean's office and the Registrar.

Ang passing grade sa CvSU ay 3.0 (75%). Gumagamit ang CvSU ng 1.0–5.0 grading scale. Ang GWA ay kinukuha mula sa weighted average ng lahat ng iyong grades. Ang INC grade ay kailangang ayusin sa loob ng isang taon. Para sa LOA, course shifting, o retention policy, makipag-ugnayan sa iyong Dean at sa Registrar's Office. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
dormitory:
CvSU STUDENT DORMITORY:

AVAILABILITY:
- Dormitories available at selected campuses (including Main Campus, Indang)
- Limited slots — priority given to students from distant areas
- Apply early — slots fill quickly during enrollment

APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS:
- Certificate of Registration (proof of enrollment)
- Medical / health clearance
- Barangay clearance
- 2x2 ID photos
- Parent/guardian consent letter
- Accomplished application form (from OSAS)

RATES:
- Fees vary per campus and room type
- Basic utilities usually included; electricity may be charged separately

RULES:
- Curfew strictly enforced
- No outside visitors after curfew
- Governed by dormitory policies

CONTACT: Office of Student Affairs & Services (OSAS)
Email: osas@cvsu.edu.ph | Visit your campus Student Affairs office.

CvSU offers student dormitories at select campuses with limited slots. To apply, get an application form from the OSAS office and submit: COR, medical clearance, barangay clearance, photos, and parent consent. Fees vary by campus. Curfew and visitation rules apply. Contact OSAS or your campus Student Affairs office for current availability and rates.

May dormitoryo ang CvSU sa ilang campus, kasama na ang Main Campus sa Indang. Limitado ang slots, kaya mag-apply nang maaga. Kailangan ng COR, medical clearance, barangay clearance, at pahintulot ng magulang. Para sa detalye ng bayad at patakaran, makipag-ugnayan sa OSAS office ng inyong campus. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
student_portal:
CvSU STUDENT PORTAL:

The CvSU Student Portal is your online gateway for academic services.

WHAT YOU CAN DO:
- View grades and academic records
- Online enrollment / subject registration
- Check class schedules
- View/print Certificate of Registration (COR)
- Track enrollment status

HOW TO ACCESS:
1. Go to: www.cvsu.edu.ph
2. Click 'Student Portal'
3. Log in with your student ID and password

FIRST-TIME LOGIN:
- Credentials issued during enrollment
- Default password is usually your birthdate (MMDDYYYY) or student ID
- Change your password after first login

FORGOT PASSWORD / TECHNICAL ISSUES:
Contact your campus ICT Office with your student ID.
Email: ictmain@cvsu.edu.ph (Main Campus)

For portal-related concerns, visit the ICT Office or Student Services.

Access the CvSU Student Portal via www.cvsu.edu.ph to view grades, enroll in subjects, check schedules, and download your COR. Use your student ID number and the password issued during enrollment. If you forgot your password, contact your campus ICT Office with your student ID for a reset.

Ang CvSU Student Portal ay accessible sa www.cvsu.edu.ph. Gamitin ang iyong student ID at password para mag-login. Doon mo mahahanap ang iyong grades, schedule, at COR. Kung nakalimutan mo ang password, pumunta sa ICT Office ng iyong campus na may dalang student ID. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
international_students:
CvSU INTERNATIONAL / FOREIGN STUDENT ADMISSIONS:

CvSU accepts foreign nationals subject to CHED and university regulations.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Accomplished application form
- Authenticated/apostilled academic credentials (HS diploma, transcripts)
- Passport copy (biodata page)
- Student Visa (9F) — apply at Bureau of Immigration AFTER admission
- English proficiency proof (if from non-English speaking country)
- Medical/health clearance
- Equivalent police clearance (NBI or from home country)

PROCESS:
1. Submit application to Office of Admissions (Main Campus, Indang)
2. Await credential evaluation and Notice of Admission (NOA)
3. Process 9F Student Visa at Bureau of Immigration
4. Complete enrollment procedures

TUITION NOTE:
Free tuition under RA 10931 may NOT apply to foreign students.
Contact the Cashier's Office for applicable fees.

CONTACT:
Office of Admissions | Email: admissions@cvsu.edu.ph
Phone: (046) 415-0010 | Website: www.cvsu.edu.ph

CvSU welcomes foreign/international students subject to CHED rules. You'll need authenticated academic credentials, passport, student visa (9F), and medical clearance. Apply at the Office of Admissions in the Main Campus, Indang. Note: Free tuition (RA 10931) may not apply to foreign nationals. Contact admissions@cvsu.edu.ph for full details.

Yes, CvSU accepts international students! Requirements include apostilled academic records, passport, and a 9F student visa processed after admission. Apply at the Office of Admissions, Main Campus, Indang, Cavite. For tuition and specific requirements, contact admissions@cvsu.edu.ph or call (046) 415-0010.
online_programs:
CvSU ONLINE & FLEXIBLE LEARNING PROGRAMS:

ETEEAP (Expanded Tertiary Education Equivalency and Accreditation Program):
- For working professionals seeking a college degree through prior learning recognition
- Credits work experience, training, and non-formal education toward a degree
- Available for select programs — inquire at the Office of Academic Affairs
- Requirements: Work portfolio, assessment/evaluation, applicable fees

FLEXIBLE / BLENDED LEARNING:
- Some programs offer hybrid delivery (face-to-face + online components)
- Availability varies per campus and program per semester
- Requires internet access and compatible device

MODULAR DISTANCE LEARNING (MDL):
- Learning modules delivered digitally or in print
- Available for select programs as approved by CHED

HOW TO APPLY FOR ETEEAP:
1. Inquire at the Office of Academic Affairs (Main Campus)
2. Submit your educational & professional portfolio
3. Undergo assessment and evaluation
4. Enroll if qualified

CONTACT:
Office of Academic Affairs | Email: academic@cvsu.edu.ph
Website: www.cvsu.edu.ph

CvSU offers flexible learning options including ETEEAP for working professionals who want to earn a degree through prior learning recognition. Select programs also offer blended/hybrid and modular distance learning. For ETEEAP, inquire at the Office of Academic Affairs at the Main Campus. Visit www.cvsu.edu.ph for the latest program offerings.

Oo, may flexible learning programs ang CvSU! Ang ETEEAP ay para sa mga nagtatrabaho na gustong makakuha ng degree batay sa kanilang karanasan. May iba ring programs na nag-aalok ng blended o modular learning. Para sa ETEEAP, makipag-ugnayan sa Office of Academic Affairs sa Main Campus. Salamat sa inyong tanong!
